sanskrit:
क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति।
कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति॥
transliteration:
kṣipraṃ bhavati dharmātmā śaśvac-chāntiṃ nigacchati |
kaunteya pratijānīhi na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśyati ||
literal_meaning: Quickly he becomes righteous, attains lasting peace; O Kaunteya, declare: My devotee never perishes.
contextual_meaning: Even if a person slips morally, continuous remembrance of the Divine rapidly re-orients the psyche toward ethical living and inner calm; the core Self remains indestructible.
psychological_state: Post-relapse resilience; the mind learns that one-pointed focus on a benevolent higher value can override guilt and re-establish moral identity within weeks.
trigger_scenario: After a binge-eating or porn relapse, the client believes 'I’m broken forever.' The therapist affirms: 'Consistent nightly journaling of gratitude to something bigger than the urge rewires the brain; you are not your lapse.'
anchor_text: Remember God and you’ll quickly return to goodness and peace.
